title: CC and BCC
drDigitalIntro: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of an email to someone in addition to the main recipient. Everyone can see who was CC'd. BCC (Blind Carbon Copy) sends a copy to someone without anyone else knowing they got it — the recipient isn't shown to others. Use CC to keep people informed and BCC when you want someone to see an email privately. To and Reply All is not the same — learn the difference to avoid embarrassing mistakes.
drDigitalSuccess: You understand when to use CC and BCC!
drDigitalHint: CC = everyone sees them. BCC = secret copy.
